author: lOrm
content:
Already-fixed by #445 (point 2). Forensic evidence:

src/mcp.rs:359-392 — StdioTransport::request reads stdout via:
  let cap = (MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE as u64).saturating_add(1);
  let bytes_read = (&mut *reader)
      .take(cap)
      .read_until(b'\n', &mut buf)
      .await ...
  if buf.len() > MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE && !buf.ends_with(b"\n") { return Err(...) }

The QA-mandated refactor (BufReader::take(MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE as u64).read_until(b'\n', ...) against a capped buffer, bail during read) is in place verbatim. The size limit is enforced BEFORE allocation, not after — the buffer can hold at most MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE + 1 bytes.

Grep evidence (src/mcp.rs):
  L52  Fix #445 point 2 — bound BEFORE allocation on the response line.
  L348 Fix #445 point 2: bound BEFORE allocation.
  L364 let cap = (MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE as u64).saturating_add(1);
  L366 .take(cap)
  L367 .read_until(b'\n', &mut buf)

Test coverage (already present, all passing):
  - fix445_oversized_line_rejected_before_full_buffering — spawns sh that emits MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE + 1 MiB of zeros, asserts the request fails with "exceeded ... without newline" and completes within 30 s (would OOM or hang with unbounded read_line).
  - fix445_normal_line_succeeds — sanity round-trip.
  - fix445_stderr_drained_does_not_deadlock, fix445_stderr_drain_populates_ring_buffer, fix445_concurrent_drain_and_request_no_deadlock — companion #445 point-1 coverage.

Verification: cargo test --lib mcp::tests::fix445 -> 5/5 pass. Full suite cargo test --lib -> 1412 pass (baseline). cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings -> exit 0.

Note: the HTTP-side concern raised in the original report (HttpTransport::request unbounded response.json()) is a SEPARATE, distinct defect — not in #445's scope, not in #680's title scope. Filing as a follow-up would be appropriate but is outside this issue's mandated location (StdioTransport::request, src/mcp.rs:181-238).
